data.title: Solana spot ETF saw net inflows of $127.9 million over the past week, while Bitwise's BSOL saw inflows of $86.3 million.
data.description: SolanaFloor reported on its X platform that Solana spot ETFs saw net inflows of $127.9 million over the past week, bringing the cumulative net inflow to $510 million. Among them, Bitwise's Solana spot ETF BSOL saw inflows of $86.3 million, exceeding the total inflows of all other Solana ETFs combined.

data.title: Aerodrome Finance Hit by 'Front-End' Attack, Users Urged to Avoid Main Domain
data.description:
Aerodrome Finance has reported that it has fallen victim to a 'front-end' attack, prompting urgent warnings for users to steer clear of the main domain to protect their assets and personal information. As security measures are reviewed and implemented, the project team is working diligently to address the vulnerabilities and restore full functionality while ensuring user safety remains a top priority.
source: https://www.coindesk.com/web3/2025/11/22/aerodrome-finance-hit-by-front-end-attack-users-urged-to-avoid-main-domain

data.title: UK Crime Network, Worth Billions, Used Crypto to Funnel Drug Cash to Russia, NCA Says
data.description:
According to a recent report from the UK's National Crime Agency (NCA), a sophisticated crime network operating within the United Kingdom, which is believed to be valued at billions of pounds, has been utilizing cryptocurrency as a means to transfer illicit drug-related profits to Russia. This revelation highlights the increasing intersection of organized crime and digital currencies, raising concerns about the effectiveness of law enforcement efforts in combating such transnational criminal activities.
source: https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/11/22/uk-crime-network-worth-billions-used-crypto-to-funnel-drug-cash-to-russia-nca-says

data.title: XRP Drops Toward Multi-Week Lows With Extreme Fear Dominating Traders
data.description:
XRP continues to struggle across the board as the broader crypto market remains under pressure. With sentiment readings stuck in extreme fear, key moving averages trending downward, and price momentum weakening on multiple timeframes, buyers appear hesitant to step in despite a slight stabilization around the mid-$1. 90s
source: https://www.ethnews.com/xrp-drops-toward-multi-week-lows-with-extreme-fear-dominating-traders/

data.title: Whale Sells Significant Amount of Wrapped Bitcoin
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Lookonchain monitoring has revealed that a whale has sold 700.2 Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) over the past two days, valued at $59.95 million. This whale had accumulated these bitcoins between May 12 and July 24, when they were worth $81.64 million, with an average price of $116,593.

data.title: Forward Industries transferred 1.727 million SOL tokens, worth $219 million, to the wallet "552ptg".
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Forward Industries transferred 1.727 million SOL tokens, worth $219.32 million, to the wallet 552ptg. Forward Industries previously purchased 6.834506 million SOL tokens at an average price of $232.08, for a total cost of $868 million. Currently, it has an unrealized loss of $718 million, representing a loss of 45%.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Collins: There are still reasons to be cautious about a December rate cut.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Janet Collins stated that she remains inclined to oppose a rate cut next month, given the continued risks to the Fed's dual mandate of inflation and employment. During a conference, Collins said, "I do think caution is needed regarding a rate cut at the December FOMC meeting. After a cumulative 50 basis point cuts in September and October, I personally believe that current policy is in a moderately tightening range, which is appropriate for the current economic conditions." (Jinshi)

data.title: Market Faces Challenges Amid Economic Reports and Holiday Impact
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, this week has been particularly challenging for risk assets, with U.S. stocks under significant pressure. Despite Nvidia's strong earnings report and optimistic comments from its CEO, the negative trend persisted. The cryptocurrency market fared even worse, with Bitcoin experiencing an 18% drop, marking its steepest weekly decline since mid-November 2022.

Key economic events to watch in the coming week (all times in UTC+8) include:

- Tuesday 21:30: U.S. September retail sales monthly rate and U.S. September PPI.

- Wednesday 9:00: Reserve Bank of New Zealand's interest rate decision.

- Wednesday 21:30: U.S. initial jobless claims for the week ending November 22.

- Thursday 03:00: Federal Reserve's Beige Book on economic conditions.

- Thursday (time TBD): Bank of Korea's interest rate decision.

- Friday: New York Fed President Williams will deliver a speech.

Jefferson's insights are potentially informative, as his views often align closely with those of Federal Reserve Chair Powell. Since joining the Fed three years ago, Jefferson's voting record has consistently matched Powell's. It is noteworthy that due to the Thanksgiving holiday in the U.S. on Thursday and the early market closure on "Black Friday," next week's trading days will be shortened, leading to significantly reduced market liquidity.

data.title: Satoshi Nakamoto losses $47 billion in Bitcoin amid intense crypto correction
data.description:
Satoshi Nakamoto’s legendary Bitcoin fortune just took a massive hit, with the wallet dropping $47 billion from its all-time high and falling to roughly $90. 7 billion. This happened during a violent market correction that blindsided traders across the United States and Asia, which dragged Bitcoin down to about $80,500 on Friday, putting the token on […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/bitcoins-satoshi-nakamoto-losses-47-billion/

data.title: Chinese owner Wingtech says Dutch intervention at Nexperia lacks legal basis, launches appeal
data.description:
Chinese tech firm Wingtech has pushed back hard against the Dutch government’s move to take control of chipmaker Nexperia, saying the decision has no legal ground and must be reversed. Wingtech had filed its first challenge around a month ago, on October 21, then expanded the case on November 10, and according to Reuters, the […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/appeal-dutch-intervention-nexperia-not-legal/
